signals.title: Canvas hack: What we know about apparent cyberattack that impacted thousands of schools
signals.summary: ShinyHunters breached Canvas LMS affecting 30M+ users across 8,000+ institutions during finals week. Names, emails, student IDs compromised. Multiple universities postponed final exams.
signals.relevance: Massive data breach directly impacted students during finals — highly relatable campus experience
signals.reliability: CNN direct reporting with Instructure confirmation

signals.title: Canvas data breach rattles colleges during finals period
signals.summary: NPR coverage of Canvas breach highlighting student impact during finals. Agreement reached May 11 with hackers returning data.
signals.relevance: Corroborating source showing national scope of breach impact on students
signals.reliability: NPR — trusted public media outlet with direct reporting

signals.title: FAS Could Lay Off 25 Percent of Staff in Summer Administrative Restructuring
signals.summary: Harvard FAS plans to lay off up to 25% of staff to close $365M structural deficit, developed with McKinsey. Grad admissions halved, non-tenure faculty budgets slashed.
signals.relevance: Harvard budget cuts directly affect grad student admissions and campus services — signals broader higher ed financial stress
signals.reliability: Harvard Crimson — primary campus newspaper with direct access to administration

signals.title: Harvard has a $57 billion endowment. It's still eyeing staff layoffs
signals.summary: Boston Globe highlights the paradox of Harvard's $57B endowment alongside massive staff layoffs, driven by endowment tax hike and deferred maintenance costs.
signals.relevance: Highlights wealth-vs-austerity paradox that resonates with students concerned about tuition value
signals.reliability: Major regional newspaper with investigative reporting

signals.title: Job Market Challenges for 2026 Graduates: Internship Shortage and High Competition
signals.summary: Internship postings fell below 2019 levels. Average 109 applications per internship on Handshake (273 in tech, 192 in finance). Grads with internship experience: 81.6% hired in 3 months vs 40.7% without.
signals.relevance: Hard data on the internship squeeze directly affecting current students' career prospects
signals.reliability: Data aggregator citing Handshake and NACE statistics

signals.title: The job market is so tough, young people are struggling just to land internships
signals.summary: CNN reports on the intensifying internship squeeze: tech postings dropped 30%, professional services 42%. Applications per posting nearly doubled.
signals.relevance: Major outlet confirming the internship crisis with specific sector-level data
signals.reliability: CNN Business — credible business reporting

signals.title: Class of 2026: A depressed hires rate is a major cause of labor market weakness for young college graduates
signals.summary: EPI reports 9.7% unemployment rate for recent college grads — same as high school diploma holders. College grad job-finding advantage at historic low.
signals.relevance: Official economic research showing the college degree advantage is shrinking to historic lows
signals.reliability: Economic Policy Institute — respected nonpartisan research organization

signals.title: Entry-Level Jobs Are Disappearing: What New Grads Need to Do Differently in 2026
signals.summary: 35% of 'entry-level' jobs now require 3+ years experience. Finance/info services lost 9,000 jobs/month since 2023. 43% of grads aged 22-27 are underemployed.
signals.relevance: Quantifies the entry-level paradox with specific data points students can relate to
signals.reliability: Industry blog citing BLS and employer survey data

signals.title: Testing Policy Update
signals.summary: Columbia announces SAT/ACT scores required starting fall 2027 applicants, ending test-optional era. Last Ivy League school to make this move.
signals.relevance: All 8 Ivy League schools now require standardized tests again — signals end of test-optional era
signals.reliability: Official university announcement — primary authoritative source

signals.title: Columbia to end test-optional admissions, the last Ivy League school to do so
signals.summary: Coverage of Columbia's decision as the final Ivy to end test-optional policy, following Princeton's October 2025 reinstatement.
signals.relevance: Broader media coverage confirming significance of the policy shift across all Ivies
signals.reliability: Established regional newspaper

signals.title: To avoid accusations of AI cheating, college students are turning to AI
signals.summary: Students use AI 'humanizer' tools to bypass AI detectors. Turnitin claims 98% accuracy but studies show 39.5% actual accuracy. False positives disproportionately affect non-native English speakers. Lawsuits filed.
signals.relevance: Paradoxical arms race between AI detection and evasion directly impacts every student's academic life
signals.reliability: NBC News — major national outlet with direct student interviews

signals.title: A Palo Alto high schooler was accused of AI cheating. His family filed a civil rights suit
signals.summary: Family filed civil rights lawsuit after student falsely accused of AI cheating by detection tools. Highlights growing legal challenges to AI detection reliability.
signals.relevance: Legal precedent for false AI cheating accusations — students watching closely
signals.reliability: SF Standard — local investigative outlet

signals.title: US to end 'Duration of Status' for F, J, and I visas and limit the time international students can study in the US
signals.summary: DHS submitted final rule May 5 to end Duration of Status for F/J visas. 4-year max admission, grace period cut from 60 to 30 days. Expected effective September 2026.
signals.relevance: Massive policy change directly affecting every international student's timeline and planning
signals.reliability: ICEF Monitor — authoritative international education intelligence source

signals.title: Intl. Students Still in Limbo, Even With USCIS Pause Over
signals.summary: Court blocked USCIS policies pausing immigration benefits for 39 countries on June 5, but international students remain in uncertainty about OPT and status.
signals.relevance: Shows ongoing uncertainty for international students even after court ruling
signals.reliability: Inside Higher Ed — leading higher education trade publication

signals.title: 7.8 Million Student Loan Borrowers Are About to Deal With a New Debt Collector: The U.S. Treasury
signals.summary: Treasury taking over $179B in defaulted student loans from 7.8M borrowers. Defaulted balances jumped from $117.3B to $179B in one quarter. Bureau of Fiscal Service lost 40% of workforce.
signals.relevance: Directly affects millions of student borrowers — Treasury collection is more aggressive than Education Dept
signals.reliability: The College Investor — established personal finance site focused on student debt

signals.title: Transition of Servicing Defaulted Federal Student Loans to the Department of the Treasury
signals.summary: Congressional Research Service report detailing the transfer of defaulted student loan servicing to Treasury's Bureau of the Fiscal Service. 2017 test found Treasury resolved defaults at lower rate.
signals.relevance: Official government report providing authoritative data on the loan transfer
signals.reliability: Congressional Research Service — nonpartisan government research arm

signals.title: The New University Side Hustle? An On-Campus Consulting Firm
signals.summary: Student-run consulting firms are growing on campuses. Illinois Business Consulting: 250 students, 22 projects for 19 companies in Spring 2026. Some serve Fortune 500 clients.
signals.relevance: Actionable alternative career path — students building real consulting experience on campus
signals.reliability: U.S. News — respected national media outlet

signals.title: 5 Takeaways From Recent Research on Student Loneliness
signals.summary: 64.7% of college students report feeling lonely. Lonely students 4x more likely to experience severe psychological distress. 40% considered withdrawing due to emotional stress.
signals.relevance: Quantified loneliness epidemic on campuses — highly relatable and shareable for students
signals.reliability: Inside Higher Ed reporting on peer-reviewed research

signals.title: Largest Gift in Drexel's History Establishes Nick Howley College of Engineering and Computing
signals.summary: Drexel receives $112.6M donation — $76M for scholarships and student programs, $36M+ for new robotics/aerospace labs. Creates Nick Howley College of Engineering and Computing.
signals.relevance: Massive investment in engineering education with direct student scholarship impact
signals.reliability: Official university press release

signals.title: College Dorms Are Terrible—and More Expensive Than Ever
signals.summary: Average dorm room $8,196 in AY2025-26, room+board $15,068. Costs doubled in 30 years at public universities. UT Knoxville rented Holiday Inn for overflow. Quality declining as costs rise.
signals.relevance: Dorm costs and quality directly affect every on-campus student — high engagement potential
signals.reliability: The Nation — established national magazine with investigative focus
